meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the no-portfolio hurdle with this intermediate-level Antigravity project template. Use Claude Code to build professional-grade AI tools today.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap is solved by shipping high-utility, AI-driven micro-SaaS projects rather than tutorials. By leveraging the Antigravity template, you automate boilerplate infrastructure, allowing you to focus on unique business logic. This approach transforms your GitHub from a collection of code snippets into a showcase of production-ready, scalable AI applications.
content.intro:
You stare at a blank GitHub profile, the silence of your commit history echoing the anxiety of the job market. You've completed the tutorials, but the industry doesn't hire students who follow instructions; they hire engineers who solve problems. The gap between your current skills and a job offer isn't effort—it's evidence.

Antigravity is the bridge. It provides the architectural scaffolding that allows you to skip the tedious plumbing of SaaS development and move straight to the logic that matters. Paired with Claude Code, you aren't just writing scripts; you are orchestrating an AI-powered development workflow that forces you to think like a Senior Engineer.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Initialize your project using the core template to ensure your environment is pre-configured for modern, serverless deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity base template, ensuring TypeScript and Next.js 14+ are configured for a production-ready SaaS.

steps.title: Integrate AI Logic with Claude
steps.description: Inject intelligent functionality into your app by defining clear, purpose-driven AI service layers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular AI service layer that handles prompt chaining and structured JSON outputs for a summarization engine.

steps.title: Configure Database and State
steps.description: Set up a lightweight, robust database schema that handles user sessions and project data without complex overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Prisma schema for the user model and project storage, including relations for AI-generated assets.

steps.title: Implement API Rate Limiting
steps.description: Professional SaaS apps require security. Implement rate limiting to protect your API keys and project integrity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Redis-based rate limiting on all primary API routes to prevent abuse and manage costs.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Take your project live to create a verifiable proof of work for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration for Vercel, ensuring environment variables are encrypted and build targets are optimized.

pro_tips: Always wrap your AI calls in a circuit breaker pattern to prevent cascading failures in your UI.
Use 'dry-run' flags in Claude Code to review proposed architectural changes before they touch your file system.
Document your 'Why' in the README; recruiters care more about the architectural decisions than the code itself.
Inject telemetry in your early builds; seeing real-world usage patterns is the single greatest learning experience.
Keep your git commits atomic; each commit should represent a single, functional improvement to your project's state.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, add a 'Case Study' tab to your portfolio site that details the specific challenges you solved during the build process.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Build, ship, and iterate until your portfolio commands respect. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
